sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: VfL Wolfsburg, sitting at 12th in the league with 39 points, host TSG Hoffenheim, who are 15th with 31 points, in a crucial Bundesliga encounter at the Volkswagen Arena in Wolfsburg. This matchup promises to be intriguing as both teams are looking to improve their standings. The referee for the game, Matthias Jollenbeck, averages 4.0 yellow cards per game and 0.17 red cards per game, suggesting a potentially disciplined yet firm officiating style.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, Wolfsburg have dominated with 3 wins, while Hoffenheim managed just 1 victory, with 1 draw in between. Their most recent encounter on January 11, 2025, saw Wolfsburg triumph 1-0 away from home, with Denis Vavro earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive 8.5 Sofascore rating. Head-to-head trends show high-scoring affairs, with 9 out of the last 10 matches featuring over 2.5 goals, and both teams scoring in 8 of those 10 games.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Struggles
sections.text: Wolfsburg are in a rough patch, currently on an 8-game winless streak and without a clean sheet in their last 12 matches. They have also been the first to concede in 7 of their last 8 games, indicating defensive vulnerabilities. Hoffenheim, on the other hand, are also struggling, with no wins in their last 3 matches and without a clean sheet in the same period. Additionally, Hoffenheim have been the first to concede in 5 of their last 7 games, and their away form shows both teams scoring in 5 of their last 6 matches.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - VfL Wolfsburg
sections.text: For Wolfsburg, forward Mohamed El Amine Amoura has been a standout with 10 goals and 8 assists this season, overperforming his expected goals (xG) of 8.66 by 1.34. Another key player, Jonas Wind, has a solid record against Hoffenheim with an average Sofascore rating of 7.2, 1 goal, and 2 assists in his last 5 starts against them. Defender Denis Vavro, ranked 4th in the league for percentage of long balls in total passes at 10%, also brings composure with a 7.16 average rating. Midfielder Patrick Wimmer adds flair with 3 goals and 4 assists, though he slightly underperforms his xG.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - TSG Hoffenheim
sections.text: Hoffenheim’s attack is led by Andrej Kramarić, who has 11 goals and 8 assists this season, closely matching his xG of 11.60, and is in form with 3 goals and 3 assists in his last 5 matches, boasting a 7.64 average Sofascore rating. Midfielder Finn Ole Becker stands out defensively, ranked 1st in the league for aerial duels won at 77.8%. Forward Adam Hlozek also impresses with 8 goals, overperforming his xG of 5.33 by 2.67, while Tom Bischof contributes creatively with 5 goals and 2 assists, overperforming his xG significantly.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Statistics
sections.text: Wolfsburg have scored 53 goals this season, ranking 7th in the league, but underperform their total expected goals of 45.77 by 7.23, placing them 15th in xG over/underperformance. Their defense struggles, conceding 52 goals (12th in least goals conceded), and they’ve only managed 5 clean sheets (15th). Hoffenheim’s goal distribution shows strength late in games, with 20.97% of their goals coming between the 61st and 75th minutes. Wolfsburg, meanwhile, peak late as well, with 23.08% of their goals scored between the 61st and 75th minutes, suggesting a potential for late drama.

sections.subtitle: Set-Piece and Discipline Trends
sections.text: Wolfsburg rank 5th in the league for headers scored with 9, which could be a weapon against Hoffenheim’s defense. They average 4.69 corners per game (9th in the league), and historically, 8 of their last 10 head-to-heads with Hoffenheim have seen less than 10.5 corners. On the discipline front, Wolfsburg have accumulated 75 yellow cards (17th for least yellow cards), averaging 2.34 per game, while head-to-head data shows 8 of the last 9 matches between these teams had less than 4.5 cards.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players and Squad Depth
sections.text: Wolfsburg will miss key midfielder Maximilian Arnold, who has 3 goals and 5 assists with a 7.13 average rating, potentially impacting their midfield control. Defender Sebastiaan Bornauw’s absence might further weaken their backline. Hoffenheim are without defender Valentin Gendrey, who has contributed 1 goal and 1 assist, which could affect their defensive stability. Both teams will need to rely on squad depth to fill these gaps.
